# TASK_BRIEF_2026-07-01_p0-04-clown-controller.md
# Game: Send in the Clowns | Codename: clowns | Phase: 0 (solo combat toy)
# Generated by: technical_designer_agent (Mission Control)
# Route: Cursor + Unity MCP (prefab/scene/components) + Claude Code CLI (C#)
# Status: ACTIVE | Depends on: TB-03
# Format: conforms to intent of CLAUDE_PROJECT_RULES.md §7.2.

## 0. Objective
Give the player a clown: LoL-style top-down camera, right-click NavMesh move, an auto-attack
baseline gag, and **two** ability "jokes" on cooldown — each a `JokeSO` carrying a humor type —
that fire through the **cast-event** path into the enemy's mirth meter (never a direct mutation).

## 1. Why this brief locks NO design choice
- Abilities are `JokeSO` **data** (humor type + archetype tag + base fill hint + cooldown +
  performance-signature ref). Swapping which jokes the clown carries = swap SO refs, no code.
- The clown's kit does not decide the roster, the class identity (Pillar 5 comes with more clowns
  later), or which humor types "win" — it only routes casts to the read.
- Auto-attack humor type is itself a `JokeSO` ref, so "what is the safe default gag" stays data.

## 2. Co-op-forward seams honored
- **Seam 4 (Input→Intent→Apply):** `ClownInput` reads mouse/keys into an `IntentFrame
  { Vector3? moveTarget; int? castIndex; }`. `ClownMover` consumes intent → `NavMeshAgent`.
  `ClownCaster` consumes intent → emits `CastEvent`. A network layer later fills `IntentFrame`
  from remote input; client-auth movement (`DEC-2026-06-29-B`) stays drop-in.
- **Seam 3 (ownership):** clown has an `ownerId` (local player in solo). The mover only runs for
  the owned clown — mirrors "owner runs its own clown's agent" without any netcode.
- **Seam 2:** casting emits `CastEvent` to the target's `MirthMeter.ResolveCast` via the
  `ActorRegistry` — the same call a broadcast will drive later. No direct meter poking.
- **Seam 5:** clown registers itself in `ActorRegistry`; no `Player.Instance`.

## 3. Prerequisites
- [ ] TB-03 complete (`MirthMeter.ResolveCast`, `ActorRegistry`, events exist).
- [ ] `ToyArena` has baked NavMesh (TB-01).

## 4. Files to create
`Assets/_clowns/Runtime/Data/`:
- `JokeSO.cs` — `ScriptableObject`. Fields: `HumorTypeSO humor`, `string archetypeTag`
  (e.g. "one-liner"/"pratfall" — a tag, NOT a joke), `float cooldown`, `float range`,
  `PerformanceSignatureSO signature` (nullable; wired in TB-05). Optional `float baseFillHint`
  (only if you want per-joke fill; default read from `MirthConfigSO`).

`Assets/_clowns/Runtime/Clown/`:
- `IntentFrame.cs` — the intent struct (Seam 4).
- `ClownInput.cs` — reads right-click (raycast to NavMesh → `moveTarget`) and ability keys
  (1/2 or Q/W → `castIndex`) into `IntentFrame`. Owner-gated.
- `ClownMover.cs` — applies `moveTarget` to `NavMeshAgent.SetDestination`. Owner-gated.
- `ClownCaster.cs` — holds `JokeSO autoAttack` + `JokeSO[] abilities` (size 2) + per-slot cooldown
  timers. On `castIndex`, if off cooldown & target in range, builds a `CastEvent {casterId=ownerId,
  humor=joke.humor, targetId, t=Time.time}` and calls the target `MirthMeter.ResolveCast`. Auto-attack
  auto-fires at nearest in-range enemy on its own timer. Raises a local `CastStartedEvent` (for TB-05
  wind-up timing) BEFORE resolution.
- `ClownActor.cs` — glue: `ownerId`, registers in `ActorRegistry`, references the above.

`Assets/_clowns/Runtime/Core/`:
- `CastStartedEvent.cs` — `struct { int casterId; JokeSO joke; int targetId; float t; }` (feedback wind-up).

`Assets/_clowns/GameData/`:
- `JK_AutoGag` (JokeSO, humor = a safe default, e.g. Wit or a neutral gag — tunable),
  `JK_Ability_A`, `JK_Ability_B` (humor types chosen so the player has real read choices vs the
  seeded temperaments — e.g. one Wit, one Slapstick, plus auto = Dark/neutral; designer-tunable).

## 5. Ordered steps
1. **[CLI]** Author `JokeSO`, `IntentFrame`, the four `Clown/` scripts, `CastStartedEvent`.
2. **[Cursor]** Build a grey-box clown prefab (capsule + `NavMeshAgent` + `ClownActor` +
   `ClownInput`/`ClownMover`/`ClownCaster`). Set `ownerId = 0` (local).
3. **[Cursor]** Add a top-down camera (fixed pitch, follows clown) — LoL/`ONE_PAGE_GDD` control feel.
4. **[CLI]** Create the three `JokeSO` assets; assign to the clown's caster (auto + 2 abilities).
5. **[Cursor]** Place clown + one TB-03 enemy in `ToyArena`. Wire the `ActorRegistry` bootstrap
   (a scene `GameBootstrap` object that registers actors on Start).
6. **[CLI]** `git commit -m "Phase 0 TB-04: clown controller — click-move + 2 jokes via cast events"`.

## 6. Guardrails (restate — non-negotiable)
- `archetypeTag` and any ability name are MECHANICAL labels only — never a punchline/joke line
  (`DEC-2026-06-29-C`). If a `JokeSO` needs written comedy to make sense, it's wrong — reject.
- Casting emits an event; it must NEVER reach into `MirthMeter.current` directly (co-op seam).
- Movement is client-side/local; do NOT add server-authority or prediction (out of Phase 0 by §6).

## 7. Verification (USER confirms)
- [ ] Right-click moves the clown via NavMesh; camera tracks top-down.
- [ ] Keys 1/2 fire the two abilities on cooldown; auto-attack fires on its timer at the nearest enemy.
- [ ] Each cast changes the enemy's mirth bar consistent with TB-03 (Strong fills big, Weak bombs).
- [ ] Casts go through `ResolveCast` (verify via a log at the resolver, not the caster).

## 8. Failure modes → diagnosis → fix
- *Clown won't move* → NavMesh not baked / agent off-mesh; rebake, snap agent to surface.
- *Cast does nothing* → target not in `ActorRegistry`, or range check failing; verify bootstrap
  registration and `JokeSO.range`.
- *Cooldowns feel off* → they're `JokeSO` data; tune `cooldown`, no recompile.
- *Input runs on a non-owned actor* → owner-gate `ClownInput`/`Mover`/`Caster` on `ownerId==localId`.

## 9. Exit gate (binary)
Solo, the player moves and fires 3 gags/jokes that register on the enemy's mirth meter through the
event path, with cooldowns and range respected. → unlocks TB-05.

## 10. Out of scope for this brief
Land/bomb visuals & audio (TB-05), temperament tells & spawning (TB-06), a second clown/co-op,
weapon-loot/archetype firing profiles (`WEAPON_SYSTEM`, backlog), combos.
